steps.title: Spot what the second sentence does
steps.content: The second sentence reveals the specific form of the tribute mentioned in the first sentence—a concrete detail that makes the general claim precise. The transition must signal this movement from general to specific.

steps.title: Match the transition to this movement
steps.content:
The logical relationship between the two sentences is specification. The first sentence makes a general claim (the band named themselves in tribute to another band); the second reveals the precise form that tribute took (the specific song title). The blank needs a transition signaling movement from general to specific.
  • Choice A — "Regardless,": signals that the second sentence holds true despite some obstacle or contrary fact. No obstacle is mentioned. ✗
  • Choice B — "Last of all,": signals the final item in a sequence. No prior list is being completed here. ✗
  • Choice C — "Instead,": signals that the second sentence replaces or contradicts the first. The second sentence confirms and specifies the tribute — it doesn't contradict it. ✗
  • Choice D — "Specifically,": signals that the second sentence narrows and names exactly what was generalized in the first, a perfect fit. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
